card_analysis.core_mechanic: Eminence: Dragon spells cost {1} less; whenever one or more Dragons attack, draw that many cards and put a permanent from hand onto the battlefield
triggered_abilities: Whenever one or more Dragons you control attack, draw that many cards, then you may put a permanent card from your hand onto the battlefield
static_abilities: Eminence — As long as The Ur-Dragon is in the command zone or on the battlefield, other Dragon spells you cast cost {1} less to cast

strategic_profile.game_plan_summary: Cast the best dragons in all five colors, leveraging Eminence cost reduction and the attack trigger for card advantage and free permanents. This is a conventional tribal lord strategy — the commander wants the highest-quality dragons available. Standard card evaluation heuristics apply: bigger, more impactful dragons are better.

win_conditions.description: Attack with a board of large flying dragons for lethal commander damage or regular damage
Value engine via attack trigger putting free permanents into play

build_paths.name: Dragon Tribal Goodstuff
build_paths.description: Play the best dragons across all colors with ramp to cast them early

high: Dragons with powerful ETB or attack triggers
Ramp spells that accelerate to 5+ mana

anti_synergies.description: Low-impact small dragons dilute the quality of attack triggers
anti_synergies.reasoning: The Ur-Dragon wants each dragon to carry significant impact; small utility dragons waste attack trigger draws

strategic_constraints.mana_base_requirements: Five-color mana base with heavy ramp; needs all five colors reliably by turn 4-5

power_indicators.notes: Very powerful when ramping into dragons, but slow and vulnerable to aggro before setup
